popularStory.headline: Memorial
popularStory.description:
David of Scotland (Medieval Gaelic: Dabíd) (c. 1144 – 17 June 1219) was a Scottish prince and Earl of Huntingdon. He was a claimant to the Scottish throne.
He was the youngest surviving son of Henry …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Ada Huntingdon於1195年出生在英國亨廷格多史爾亨廷格頓時，她的父親David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon51歲，母親Matilda of Chester24歲。她於1237年6月7日之前與Henry de Hastings結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1242年在英國英格蘭爾享年48歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭爾阿斯特伯里。

nameMeanings.name: Huntingdon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Huntington
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places called Huntington, most of which are named with Old English huntena, the genitive plural form of hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’, + dūn ‘hill’ or tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Some, however, may have Old English hunting ‘hunting’ as the initial element, while others may derive from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan), perhaps with Old English connective -ing-, + tūn. The surname may also arise from Huntingdon (Huntingdonshire), which derives from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan) or Old English hunta (genitive huntan) + dūn.

History: A prominent American family of this name were founded by Simon Huntington, who himself never saw the New World, for he died in 1633 on the voyage to Boston, where his widow settled with her children. Their descendants include Jabez Huntington (1719–86), a wealthy West Indies trader, and Samuel Huntington (1731–96), who was one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. Collis Potter Huntington (1821–1900) was an American railway magnate. Beginning with little education or money, he made a huge fortune, some of which he left to his nephew, Henry Huntington (1850–1927), who used the money to establish the Huntington library and art gallery in CA.

nameMeanings.name: Ada
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of uncertain origin; it was not in general use before the late 18th century. It may be a Latinate variant of the biblical name Adah . However, it has also been explained as a pet form of Adele and Adelaide , Germanic female names of which the first element is adal ‘noble’. It was borne by a 7th-century abbess of Saint-Julien-des-Prés at Le Mans.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Ada Huntingdon was born in 1195, in Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, England, her father, David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon, was 51 and her mother, Matilda of Chester, was 24. She married Henry de Hastings before 7 June 1237. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died about 1242, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 48, and was buried in Astbury, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ada Huntingdon est née en 1195, à Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, son père, David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Matilda of Chester, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Henry de Hastings avant 7 juin 1237. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée environ 1242, à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterrée à Astbury, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ada Huntingdon 1195 in Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon 51 und ihre Mutter Matilda of Chester 24. Sie hat vor 7. Juni 1237 Henry de Hastings geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb um 1242 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Astbury, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lei sposato Henry de Hastings prima del 7 giugno 1237. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. ko.lifeSummary: Ada Huntingdon(이)가 1195년 영국 헌틴그도실르 헌틴그던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon(은)는 51세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda of Chester(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1237년 6월 7일 이전 Henry de Hastings(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1242년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 애스트버이에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Henry de Hastings antes 7 de junho de 1237. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ada Huntingdon родилась в 1195 г. в местности хантингдон, хантингдоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Matilda of Chester, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry de Hastings перед 7 июнь 1237 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла около 1242 г. в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 48 лет и была похоронена в местности астбури, чешир, Англия, Великобритания.
